# see — inspector capability protocol

`see` hosts uploaded static apps in a sandboxed viewer with an inspector bar that can talk to the page via an opt-in capability protocol.

---

## Opting in

Add the SDK script tag to your page's `<head>` (or end of `<body>`):

```html
<script src="/sdk/see-inspect.js" data-see-capabilities="inspect,tweaks"></script>
```

Use a root-relative URL (`/sdk/see-inspect.js`) when your content is served from the same host. If you know the absolute public URL of the host, use that instead. The script is safe to include: it communicates only via `postMessage` and the page can never script the host.

You may list one or both capabilities in `data-see-capabilities`:

- `inspect` — element inspection and screenshot
- `tweaks` — live design controls in the bar

---

## inspect capability

Mark any element you want the bar to be able to highlight, select, and screenshot:

```html
<div data-see-inspectable data-see-id="hero" data-see-label="Hero section">…</div>
```

- `data-see-inspectable` — required; marks the element as a target
- `data-see-id` — optional stable identifier for the element
- `data-see-label` — optional human-readable label shown in the bar

---

## tweaks capability

Tweaks are named design knobs (string, number, or boolean) that the bar exposes as live controls. The owner can save the current values in Settings so they persist across loads.

### Declaring defaults

Declare your tweak defaults in a single root-level inline script using edit-mode markers so the bar can parse and edit them:

```html
<script>
  const TWEAK_DEFAULTS = /*EDITMODE-BEGIN*/{ "primaryColor": "#D97757", "fontSize": 16, "dark": false, "variant": "A" }/*EDITMODE-END*/;
</script>
```

Rules:
- The value between the markers must be valid JSON with double-quoted keys and strings.
- There must be exactly one `/*EDITMODE-BEGIN*/…/*EDITMODE-END*/` block in the page.
- The bar infers control kinds from value types: `boolean` → toggle, `number` → slider/number input, a `#rrggbb` string → color picker, any other string → text input.

### Enriching controls (optional)

To customise labels, ranges, units, or control kinds, define a global `window.SeeTweaks` object keyed by tweak name:

```html
<script>
  window.SeeTweaks = {
    fontSize:     { kind: "number",  min: 12, max: 24, step: 1, unit: "px", label: "Font size", group: "Type" },
    primaryColor: { kind: "color",   cssVar: "--color-primary" },
    variant:      { kind: "select",  options: ["A", "B", "C"] },
  };
</script>
```

Supported metadata fields per tweak:

| field | description |
|-------|-------------|
| `kind` | `"toggle"`, `"number"`, `"color"`, `"text"`, `"select"` — overrides the inferred kind |
| `label` | display name shown in the bar |
| `group` | group heading in the bar panel |
| `min`, `max`, `step` | numeric range (for `kind: "number"`) |
| `unit` | unit suffix displayed next to the value (e.g. `"px"`, `"rem"`) |
| `cssVar` | CSS custom property to set automatically (e.g. `"--accent"`) — the SDK calls `document.documentElement.style.setProperty(cssVar, value)` |
| `options` | array of allowed string values (for `kind: "select"`) |

### Applying changes at runtime

When the user moves a control, the bar calls `applyLive(id, value)` if it is defined on the page:

```html
<script>
  function applyLive(id, value) {
    if (id === "dark") {
      document.body.classList.toggle("dark", value);
    } else if (id === "fontSize") {
      document.documentElement.style.setProperty("--font-size-base", value + "px");
    }
  }
</script>
```

If you set `cssVar` in `window.SeeTweaks`, the SDK automatically sets that CSS variable and you do not need to handle it in `applyLive`.

### Persistence

Tweak values persist only if the share owner explicitly saves them in Settings (via the bar or the API). Saved tweaks are injected into the viewer page as `data-tweaks` on `#staticShareRoot` and forwarded to the page by the SDK before `applyLive` is called.

---

## Editing content (for agents)

A share is a living set of files — you can patch them over time and every open viewer reloads automatically. The API (origin = the host serving this `/llms.txt`):

- `GET /api/uploads/{id}/resources` — list files (`{ resources: [{ path, bytes, contentType, … }], revision }`).
- `GET /api/uploads/{id}/resources/{path}` — fetch one file's raw contents (for reading before you patch).
- `POST /api/uploads/{id}/patch` — **structured, surgical patching** (preferred — see below).
- `PATCH /api/uploads/{id}/resources/{path}` — replace one whole file. Body is the raw new contents. Bumps the revision.
- `POST /api/uploads/{id}/resources` — add/replace files via multipart (`file`, optional `path`).
- `DELETE /api/uploads/{id}/resources/{path}` — remove a file (an index page must remain).

Authentication: send `Authorization: Bearer <password>` if the share has a password. If the owner cleared the password, edits are open (the link alone grants editing).

### Structured patching — `POST /api/uploads/{id}/patch`

Instead of re-uploading a whole file, address the exact nodes you want to change with the query language native to the file type, and batch many edits into one request. The file type is detected from the extension. Body:

```json
{
  "dryRun": false,
  "ops": [
    { "file": "index.html", "select": ".hero h1", "action": "setText", "value": "Summer Sale" },
    { "file": "index.html", "select": "#cta",      "action": "setAttr", "name": "href", "value": "/buy" },
    { "file": "index.html", "select": ".sold-out", "action": "remove" },
    { "file": "data.json",  "pointer": "/items/0/price", "action": "set", "value": 42 },
    { "file": "style.css",  "selectRule": ".btn", "action": "setDecl", "prop": "color", "value": "#D97757" }
  ]
}
```

Response: `{ ok, dryRun, revision, changed: ["index.html", …], results: [{ file, matched, applied, error? }, …] }` — one `results` entry per op, in order. `matched` is how many nodes the address hit (an HTML/CSS selector can match several — the op applies to all). Set `dryRun: true` to get match counts back **without writing** — use it to confirm your selectors first.

**Atomic & safe.** If any op is invalid (bad action, malformed address, unparseable target file, missing file), the whole batch is rejected with HTTP `422` and *nothing* is written. A selector that legitimately matches zero nodes is **not** an error — it reports `matched: 0` and is skipped. On success the revision bumps once and all viewers reload. Max 200 ops per batch.

**HTML** — `file` ends in `.html`/`.htm`, addressed by **CSS selector** (`select`):

| action | fields | effect |
|--------|--------|--------|
| `setText` | `value` | replace text content |
| `setHtml` | `value` | replace inner HTML |
| `setAttr` | `name`, `value` | set an attribute |
| `removeAttr` | `name` | remove an attribute |
| `addClass` / `removeClass` | `value` | add/remove class(es) |
| `append` / `prepend` | `value` | insert HTML inside, at end/start |
| `replaceWith` | `value` | replace the element with HTML |
| `remove` | — | delete the element |

**JSON** — `file` ends in `.json`, addressed by **RFC 6901 JSON Pointer** (`pointer`, e.g. `/items/0/price`; `""` is the whole document):

| action | fields | effect |
|--------|--------|--------|
| `set` | `value` | set/replace at the pointer (use trailing `/-` to append to an array) |
| `insert` | `value` | insert into an array at the pointer's index (shifts the rest) |
| `append` | `value` | push onto the array addressed by the pointer |
| `remove` | — | delete the key or array element |

**CSS** — `file` ends in `.css`, rules addressed by selector text (`selectRule`):

| action | fields | effect |
|--------|--------|--------|
| `setDecl` | `selectRule`, `prop`, `value` | set a declaration (adds it if absent) |
| `removeDecl` | `selectRule`, `prop` | remove a declaration |
| `removeRule` | `selectRule` | remove the whole rule |
| `addRule` | `selector`, `declarations` (object) | append a new rule |

Live updates: viewers subscribe to `GET /api/uploads/{id}/events` (Server-Sent Events). Every mutation emits `data: {"type":"update","revision":<n>}`, and the viewer reloads the iframe — so a patch you make is visible to everyone within a moment. You do not need to do anything special; just patch the files.

Workspace settings: `GET /api/uploads/{id}/settings` and `PATCH …/settings` (homepage, exposed pages, tweaks, password). See the host for details.
